title: WINTER AND SEVERE WEATHER INFORMATION SERVICES MULTNOMAH COUNTY - 211info
text: 211INFO WINTER AND SEVERE WEATHER INFORMATION SERVICES MULTNOMAH COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Seasonal winter shelters may be open in addition to severe cold weather shelters that may be activated when a severe weather notice is issued by Homeless Service Department (HSD). The criteria used to determine a severe weather notice in Multnomah County and the corresponding opening of emergency warming centers are: -Temperatures forecast at 25°F or below OR -Forecasts predict at least an inch of snow in most areas OR -Overnight temperatures forecast at 32°F or below, with at least an inch of driving rain OR -Other conditions, including severe wind chills or extreme temperature fluctuations Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Winter shelters are open throughout the winter season, and severe weather shelters may be activated during times of severe weather between November 1-March 31 each year. Hours vary. Email 211 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers 211 211info (503) 222-5555 Alternate (503) 226-3099 Administrative Line 898211 Text Short Code Main phone 211 Text Short Code 898211 Text PDXSHELTER to receive updates during severe weather Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for information on year-round, winter and severe weather shelters, donations, and shelter volunteering locations. Visit website to sign up to receive email and/or text notifications when a cold or severe weather advisory is declared in Multnomah County. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Cold Weather Advisories Community Shelters Extreme Cold Warming Centers Extreme Weather Shelters Other Services Offered At This Location: DISASTER SERVICES - 211INFO DCM LINE DISASTER SERVICES - GORGE CITIZEN ALERT PROGRAM DISASTER SERVICES - SEVERE WINTER WEATHER INFORMATION SERVICES FOOD - 211INFO SNAP AND FOOD ACCESS HOUSING/SHELTER - COORDINATED ENTRY FOR FAMILIES MULTNOMAH COUNTY HOUSING/SHELTER - MULTNOMAH COUNTY EVICTION PREVENTION PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - MULTNOMAH COUNTY FAMILY SHELTER WAITLIST INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO CHILD CARE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO COORDINATION CENTER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO ICE IMPACT SUPPORT INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO INFORMATION AND REFERRAL INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO OUTREACH AND MATERIALS REQUESTS INFORMATION SERVICES - 211INFO RESOURCE DEPARTMENT INFORMATION SERVICES - OREGON HIV/STD HOTLINE MONEY MANAGEMENT - 211INFO FINANCIAL WELLNESS UTILITIES - 211INFO UTILITY ASSISTANCE UPDATES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
